meaning: to include something as a necessary part, or to cause someone to participate
coreMeaning: to include something as a necessary part, or to cause someone to participate
coreImage: 一个贺旋黑洞，靠近就被卷进去——成为其中不可分割的一部分
fable: 有一个贺旋形黑洞立在路中间，告示牌写着：「只是路过，不会被卷入。」一个工程师好奇地往里看了一眼，发现自己已经出现在项目会议室里。他大叫：「我只是看了一眼！」黑洞平静地说：「This project involves everyone who gets close.」工程师叹气，打开电脑，开始写代码。
moral: involve = 让某人或某事成为不可分割的一部分，一旦进去就脱不了身。
memoryHook: in + volve → volve 来自拉丁语 volvere（旋转、滚动）→ involve = 把某物滚卷进去，成为其中一部分。（真实词源；evolve = 滚出来，involve = 滚进去）
anchorSentence: This project involves a lot of cross-team coordination.
collocations: be involved in a project / accident / discussion
get involved in / get the team involved
involve a lot of / involve some risk
activeChunks: be involved in the decision-making process
involve a lot of cross-team coordination
get everyone involved from the start
sentencePatterns: We need to involve ... in this process.
personalExample: My current project involves coordinating with three different teams across time zones.
notes: involve doing（正确）→ The job involves traveling. ✓。involve to do（错误）→ × The job involves to travel.。involve vs. include：include = 列入其中（静态）；involve = 包含作为必要部分或后果（动态）。be involved in 也可暗示「牵连、卷入」负面事件：be involved in a scandal。
recallQuestions: 那个只要靠近就把人卷进项目会议室的贺旋黑洞，对应哪个英文单词？
involve 后面接什么形式？to do 还是 doing？
用 involve 或 be involved in 写一句和你当前工作或项目有关的英文句子。
